RecentlyUsedContainer
HTTPHeaderDictc
    Provides a thread-safe dict-like container which maintains up to
    ``maxsize`` keys while throwing away the least-recently-used keys beyond
    ``maxsize``.
    :param maxsize:
        Maximum number of recent elements to retain.
    :param dispose_func:
        Every time an item is evicted from the container,
        ``dispose_func(value)`` is called.  Callback which will get called
    
_maxsize

Nz7Iteration over this class is unlikely to be threadsafe.)

RecentlyUsedContainer.clearc

RecentlyUsedContainer.keys)
__doc__r
d!d"
d#d$
d%d&
Z d'd(
Z!d)d*
Z"e#d+d,
    :param headers:
        An iterable of field-value pairs. Must not contain multiple field names
        when compared case-insensitively.
    :param kwargs:
        Additional field-value pairs to pass in to ``dict.update``.
    A ``dict`` like container for storing HTTP Headers.
    Field names are stored and compared case-insensitively in compliance with
    RFC 7230. Iteration provides the first case-sensitive key seen for each
    case-insensitive pair.
    Using ``__setitem__`` syntax overwrites fields that compare equal
    case-insensitively in order to maintain ``dict``'s api. For fields that
    compare equal, instead create a new ``HTTPHeaderDict`` and use ``.add``
    in a loop.
    If multiple fields that are equal case-insensitively are passed to the
    constructor or ``.update``, the behavior is undefined and some will be
    lost.
    >>> headers = HTTPHeaderDict()
    >>> headers.add('Set-Cookie', 'foo=bar')
    >>> headers.add('set-cookie', 'baz=quxx')
    >>> headers['content-length'] = '7'
    >>> headers['SET-cookie']
    'foo=bar, baz=quxx'
    >>> headers['Content-Length']
    '7'
    Nc
superr
isinstance
_copy_from
extend)
headers
kwargs
	__class__r

z(HTTPHeaderDict.__eq__.<locals>.<genexpr>)

HTTPHeaderDict.__iter__c
D.pop(k[,d]) -> v, remove specified key and return the corresponding value.
        If key is not found, d is returned if given, otherwise KeyError is raised.
        N)

_HTTPHeaderDict__marker)

HTTPHeaderDict.popc
discard
HTTPHeaderDict.discardc
Adds a (name, value) pair, doesn't overwrite the value if it already
        exists.
        >>> headers = HTTPHeaderDict(foo='bar')
        >>> headers.add('Foo', 'baz')
        >>> headers['foo']
        'bar, baz'
        N)
setdefault
append)
Z	key_lowerZ
new_valsrT
HTTPHeaderDict.addc
qHnjt
qnnDt
Generic import function for any type of header-like object.
        Adapted version of MutableMapping.update in order to insert items
        with self.add instead of self.__setitem__
        r
z9extend() takes at most 1 positional arguments ({0} given)r

zmReturns a list of all the values for the named field. Returns an
        empty list if the key doesn't exist.r
